section_title: Part 1 — Executive Summary / Overview (incl. §1.1 Overview, §1.2 How It Differs, §1.4 Preface — top-of-site framing)
scope_note: Part 1 is the top-of-site executive framing: scorecard, three-tier roadmap, and sections 1.1–1.x. It is the first prose a new reader meets. Fingerprint hunt asks: does our framing inadvertently concede ground by citing dome parameters without flagging their globe origins?
existing_fingerprints_noted: a = 20,015 km ≈ π × R_Earth (SEC-2.1 fp#2, SEC-2.9 existing list, SEC-4.9, WIN-001 fp, WIN-002 fp)
H(r) = 8,537·exp(−r/8,619) curve-fitted to WGS84 distances (SEC-2.1 fp#1)
n(r) algebraically forced to inverse-AE stretch k·colat/sin(colat) (SEC-2.9 fp#1)
1.57c ≈ π/2 phase-velocity waveguide identity (WIN-001 fp, SEC-4.9)

fingerprint_1.name: Executive Summary cites the three architectural parameters (a = 20,015 km; b ≈ 15,000 km; r_equator ≈ 10,008 km) as if they were free dome choices — all three are π·R_Earth scaled by simple fractions (1, 3/4, 1/2). Part 1 never flags this, so a reader who reads only the executive summary sees dome parameters as neutral geometric constants rather than as a direct signature of the globe being projected. Parts 2, 4, and 5 prove this later, but the fingerprint is absent where first impressions form.
fingerprint_1.kernel_of_truth: The Executive Summary is deliberately descriptive-not-argumentative in places (section 1.1 is 'Overview', explicitly summarizing the author's own architecture). Listing parameters without debunking each one is a reasonable style choice — the heavy refutation is correctly deferred to the Tier-1/2/3 structure. The author genuinely publishes these three numbers as the dome's architectural inputs, and our description at §1.1 is faithful to his own presentation. We should not over-load the Overview with critique that belongs in later tabs.
fingerprint_1.but_here_is_why_it_fails: 'Descriptive' still implies a choice of frame. Citing a = 20,015 km, b ≈ 15,000 km, and r_equator ≈ 10,008 km without a parenthetical note tacitly concedes that these are independent dome inputs. In fact all three equal π·R_Earth × {1, 3/4, 1/2} to better than 0.1%. This is the azimuthal-equidistant (AE) projection identity: center the globe on the north pole, flatten it, and the equator appears at πR/2, the 45°S parallel appears at 3πR/4, and the antipodal south pole appears at πR. The dome has not invented an elliptical disc; it has re-labelled the radial coordinates of a globe-projection map. A dome defender reading only Part 1 can frame the review as 'our critics accept his parameters and argue with his physics.' A one-sentence parenthetical in §1.1 removes that framing without doing any Tier-1 work the later parts don't already do — it simply makes the fingerprint visible where first impressions are formed.

globe_value.name: Azimuthal-equidistant (AE) projection radii from the north pole on a sphere of radius R_Earth = 6,371 km
derivation: Antipode (south pole): r = π·R = π·6,371 = 20,015.087 km
globe_value.source: Elementary AE projection: radial coordinate equals great-circle arc length from the projection pole. Standard geodesy; used in e.g. polar navigation charts and the UN emblem.

dome_value.name: Published Part 1 architectural parameters (as they appear in §1.1 'Key architectural parameters' and elsewhere)
values: Disc semi-major axis a = 20,015 km (quoted 9× in Part 1)
Equatorial radial coordinate r ≈ 10,008 km (used in §1.3 to cite n(r)≈1.44 at the equator)

match_precision.b_vs_3piR_over_4: 0.075% (15,000 vs 15,011; if the 15,037 value implied by e=0.66,a=20,015 is used instead, match improves to 0.17%)
match_precision.eccentricity: e=0.66 follows algebraically from a=πR and b=3πR/4: √(1-(3/4)²) = √(7/16) = 0.6614 — not an aesthetic choice from Hildegard's cosmos, but a derivative of two globe quantities.

quantitative_link.unified_identity: All three dome lengths are π·R_Earth multiplied by a rational fraction (1, 3/4, 1/2). The eccentricity 0.66 is then a derived quantity, not an input. This is not three coincidences — it is one identity (the AE projection) producing three consistent fingerprints.
quantitative_link.why_three_and_not_one: A single 0.0006% match (a = πR) could be dismissed as coincidence or curve-fit. Three matches at the same precision, related by simple fractions, cannot: the disc coordinate system IS the AE projection with polar scale R_Earth. The dome's ellipse is the AE image of a sphere, truncated at the south pole and lightly squashed; the squash factor (b/a = 3/4) is itself globe-derived (the 45°S parallel).

key_findings: find_curve.py literal comment 'Required H(lat) to maintain perfect WGS84 map radius (r = (90-lat)*111.1)' ties the radial coordinate r directly to globe arc length per degree of latitude (πR/180°) — already documented by SEC-2.1 fp#1 but identically supports the AE-projection interpretation of a = πR

test_8537.py uses globe city-pair great-circle distances (London-NYC 5,570 km; Stockholm-Oslo 416 km; Cape Town-Buenos Aires 6,860 km) as the fit target; these are WGS84 distances that only close on a sphere of radius R_Earth
No file in the repo derives a, b, or r_equator from any aetheric or dome-native principle; they are stated as inputs in the parameter block
e = 0.66 is not computed in the repo — it is stated; and the algebraic relationship e = √(1 − (3/4)²) = 0.6614 is not acknowledged in commentary, yet follows the moment b is fixed at 3πR/4
code_evidence.verification_method: GitHub API read of john09289/predictions repo + cross-reference with SEC-2.1 and SEC-2.9 fingerprint files already in our review

fingerprint_1.why_it_matters: Executive Summary is where the interpretive frame is set. Readers who bounce after Part 1 form an impression that the dome has its own geometry that must be evaluated on its own terms. A single parenthetical fingerprint note at §1.1 changes the frame from 'the dome has geometric inputs we will critique later' to 'the dome's geometric inputs are already globe quantities in disguise — the later sections make this explicit'. This strengthens the Tier-1/2/3 roadmap (the three levels are then ordered self-contradictions first because the parameters themselves are globe-derived, not after the author chose them) and preempts a defender reframe of Part 1.

defender_rebuttal_and_counter.defender_claim: a = 20,015 km is not π·R_Earth — that's numerology. The disc radius is set by where the ice wall is observed, which happens to be where the projection puts the antipode.
defender_rebuttal_and_counter.counter: Three simultaneous matches at ≤0.1% precision, each related to πR by a simple fraction (1, 3/4, 1/2), with the eccentricity then algebraically derived, is not numerology — it is the defining AE identity. 'The ice wall is observed at 20,015 km' is not an independent observation: the disc radius is fixed by fitting H(r) against globe city-pair distances (see SEC-2.1 fp#1) and the r(lat) calibration r = (90−lat)·111.1 km is literally the globe arc-length per degree of latitude (111.1 km ≡ πR/180°). Every step of the dome's coordinate system is AE projection in disguise.
defender_rebuttal_and_counter.defender_claim_2: The ellipse shape comes from Hildegard of Bingen's 1151 AD cosmos (Scivias). That's a historical/aesthetic choice, not a globe fingerprint.
defender_rebuttal_and_counter.counter_2: The Scivias diagram is egg-shaped with no numerical parameters. The specific numerical values a = πR, b = 3πR/4 (eccentricity 0.66) do not appear in Hildegard. They appear in the dome only after parameter values are chosen to match globe distances, at which point e = √(1 − (b/a)²) = √(7/16) = 0.661 is forced by the choice. Citing Hildegard for the shape is a historical cover; citing πR_Earth for the scale is the operational reality.

suggested_additions.target: Part 1, §1.1 'Overview' — after the 'Key architectural parameters' paragraph, before closing the <details>
suggested_additions.text: (The three radial lengths quoted above are not free dome parameters. Disc semi-major a = 20,015 km matches π·R_Earth to 0.0004%; semi-minor b ≈ 15,000 km matches 3π·R_Earth/4 (the AE-projection image of the 45°S parallel); the equatorial radial coordinate r ≈ 10,008 km matches π·R_Earth/2 (the AE-projection image of the equator). Eccentricity e = 0.66 then follows algebraically from b/a = 3/4. In other words, the dome's disc is the azimuthal-equidistant projection of a globe of radius 6,371 km, lightly squashed at the 45°S image. Parts 2, 4, and 5 develop the downstream consequences; this note flags the signature at the point where the parameters are first introduced.)
suggested_additions.rationale: Preempts the 'our critics accept his parameters' defender framing at the top of the review. Costs one paragraph and cross-references later parts rather than duplicating their arguments.
suggested_additions.tldr_review: The §1.1 TLDR currently says only that the ECM is a flat disc enclosed in a toroidal cavity. Consider adding a closing sentence: 'The three radial lengths the author publishes as dome inputs are already π·R_Earth multiplied by 1, 3/4, and 1/2 — the azimuthal-equidistant projection of a globe.' This keeps the TLDR layman-accessible while flagging the fingerprint upfront.

suggested_additions.target: Part 1, 'How This Review Is Organized' evidence-roadmap paragraph — one-line footnote or parenthetical at the end of the Tier-1 description
suggested_additions.text: (Tier 1 is the hardest to escape because even the dome's own geometric inputs — a = π·R_Earth, b = 3π·R_Earth/4, r_equator = π·R_Earth/2 — are already globe quantities; see §1.1 and Parts 2, 4.)
suggested_additions.rationale: Reinforces the fingerprint at the roadmap, where readers form their expectation of what 'self-contradiction' means. Optional if the §1.1 addition is accepted; include only one of the two if space is a concern.

potential_defender_concerns.concern: Making §1.1 argumentative could undermine its role as a faithful overview.
potential_defender_concerns.resolution: The proposed text is a parenthetical with numerical facts, not an argument. It says 'this value equals that value' and points forward to the arguments; it does not claim the dome is refuted in §1.1.

potential_defender_concerns.concern: Duplicates content that is already in Section 4.9 and SEC-2.9 lead-ins.
potential_defender_concerns.resolution: Part 1 currently has no cross-reference to those sections for the AE identity, and only Part 2 mentions πR at all (3 occurrences — all in later subsections). The §1.1 addition is upstream of any existing mention; adding it does not duplicate prose but links it.

newsworthy: The dome's three headline parameters (20,015 / 15,000 / 10,008 km) are π·R_Earth × (1, 3/4, 1/2) — executive summary currently omits this where first impressions form
cross_references: SEC-2.1 fingerprint: H(r) calibration to WGS84 distances pins 8,537 km supremum 12% below 7.83 Hz requirement
SEC-2.9 fingerprint list: explicit a=πR, b=3πR/4, e=0.66-derived enumeration — same identities, flagged at the V13 coordinate-system tab
SEC-4.9 (if present, otherwise Part 4 AE-projection discussion): full derivation of AE identity
